raw_consumer_statement:
CFPB COMPLAINT : AMEX BAD FAITH & REGULATORY DECEPTION SUBJECT : URGENT NOTICE OF DECEPTIVE PRACTICES ( CFPB Ref No : XXXX and XXXX ; AMEX Ref : XXXX and XXXX ) I am a senior citizen documenting a calculated act of Regulatory Deception and Bad Faith by American Express regarding a {$78000.00} dispute with XXXX XXXX. 

On XX/XX/year>, Amex sent a formal letter ( Ref : XXXX and XXXX explicitly inviting me to provide " written proof of damage from a professional engineer. '' Today, XX/XX/XXXX, while I was attempting to upload the requested 24-page Structural Engineering Report ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. ), Amex agents intentionally obstructed the process, closed the dispute portal mid-call, and sent a generic " Arbitration '' email instead of the promised evidence link.

This engineering report documents : Structural Failure : Forensic moisture readings of 33.3 % saturation.

Statutory Fraud : The contract is void ab initio under XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( Illegal payment schedule ) and XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ( Unregistered signer ). 

State Criminal Referral : The California Dept. of Insurance has referred this merchant to the Criminal Fraud Division ( File # XXXX ). 

Amex is utilizing administrative obstruction to prevent the delivery of forensic evidence they requested. Furthermore, Amex re-labeled this purchase as a " Cash Advance '' ( XXXX XXXX ) specifically to trigger predatory interest and evade FCBA protections. I demand a permanent reversal of this fraudulent debt.
